prompt:
Add an 'admin_chat_id' configuration option for Telegram that receives health/monitor notifications separately from the main conversation chat.

## Problem
When telegram_chat_id is 0 (auto-capture mode), the health monitor fires immediately on startup and fails because the chat ID hasn't been captured yet. Even with a configured chat_id, health alerts go to the same chat as conversations, which isn't always desired.

## Solution
Add a new config field 'telegram_admin_chat_id' that the monitor uses for health alerts. If set, health alerts go there instead of the main chat. If unset, fall back to the existing behavior (main chat ID).

## Files to change

### 1. internal/config/config.go
- Add TelegramAdminChatID int64 to ChatConfig struct (around line 69, next to TelegramChatID)
  `yaml:"telegram_admin_chat_id"`

### 2. internal/chat/telegram.go
- Add adminChatID int64 field to the Telegram struct (line ~28)
- Update NewTelegram() constructor (line ~40) to accept adminChatID parameter
- Add a new method SendAdmin(ctx, msg, threadKey) that sends to adminChatID instead of chatID
  - If adminChatID == 0, fall back to Send/SendThread (existing behavior)
  - If adminChatID is set, send directly to that chat ID (no auto-capture needed, no 'waiting for first message' error)
- Keep existing Send()/SendThread() unchanged — they still use the main chatID

### 3. internal/chat/chat.go
- Add SendAdmin(ctx context.Context, msg string, threadKey string) error to the Notifier interface
- Implement it on the Google Chat notifier too (can just delegate to SendThread as a no-op distinction)

### 4. internal/monitor/monitor.go
- In alert() (line ~92), change m.notifier.SendThread() to m.notifier.SendAdmin()
  - This routes health alerts through the admin channel

### 5. cmd/reel-life/main.go
- Pass cfg.Chat.TelegramAdminChatID to NewTelegram() (around line 91)

### 6. nix/module.nix
- Add chatTelegramAdminChatID option (near line 132), type int, default 0
  - Description: 'Telegram chat ID for admin/health notifications (0 = use main chat ID)'
- Wire it into the config generation block (around line 220)

### 7. README.md
- Add telegram_admin_chat_id to the config example, with a comment explaining its purpose

### 8. config.yaml.example (if it exists)
- Add the new field with a comment

## Acceptance criteria
- Health alerts use admin_chat_id when configured
- Health alerts fall back to main chat_id when admin_chat_id is 0
- No startup error when admin_chat_id is set but main chat_id is 0 (auto-capture)
- Existing conversation behavior unchanged
- Tests pass (run 'make test' or 'go test ./...')
- Add a test for SendAdmin routing logic
